content.full_text:
January 29, 1992

John Wallace, Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Eighth Floor  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Informal Advice

Dear John:

This letter is to confirm our telephone discussion on December 27, 1991 and your conclusion that two candidates can establish a joint "officeholder" account from which contributions to legislative candidates can be made.

The legal premises of the conclusion were: (1) under the remaining provisions of Proposition 73, a candidate may establish one committee for each election and for the expenses associated with holding that office. (Govt. Code section 89510(b) and Cal. Code of Regs. section 18520(c)); (2) an officeholder, and in particular a Minority Leader, can use officeholder account funds to make contributions to other candidates under the Political Reform Act (Advice Letter to Jennings, I-91-440); and (3) two candidates may establish a joint committee, provided it is their principal committee for that election (Advice Letter to Bott, A-91-386).

In this case, the officeholder account would be considered the 1990 committee -- for "seeking election in 1990 and the expenses associated with holding that office." Each Minority Leader would maintain his own 1992 re-elect committee.

The principal issues discussed were:

(1) whether officeholder account funds can be used for these purposes, i.e., are contributions to candidates permitted to be made under the "personal use" provisions of the Act? The Commission's Advice Letter to Jennings affirms that an officeholder account may be used by a Minority Leader for this purpose; and, (2) to whom are the contributions to the joint

John Wallace, Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
January 29, 1992  
Page Two

committee attributed? In this case, contributions to the account would be attributed to both candidates.

Both candidates are advised that this advice could change dramatically if Proposition 73 is reinstated by the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals. (Advice Letter to Bott, supra, and Advice Letter to Roberti, A-89-125.)

I would greatly appreciate your confirmation of this oral telephone advice. Please call me if you have any questions about this request.

Very truly yours,

Charles H. Bell, Jr.

CHB/man

103401
cc: The Honorable Ken Maddy
     The Honorable Bill Jones
